ddls:
CREATE TABLE "networks" (
  "network_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network */,
  "network_name" TEXT /* Name of the network */,
  "network_type" TEXT /* Type of network (e.g., WiFi, LTE, 5G) */,
  "max_capacity" TEXT /* Maximum capacity of the network in Mbps */,
  "coverage_area" TEXT /* Geographical coverage area of the network */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the service provider */,
  "technology_version" TEXT /* Version of the network technology */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("network_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, technician) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated metric entry */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "event_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the event */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the event (e.g., info, warning, critical) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who triggered the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis result */,
  "time_period" TEXT /* Time period for the analysis (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "average_bit_rate" TEXT /* Average bit rate during the time period */,
  "average_delay" TEXT /* Average delay experienced during the time period */,
  "total_bytes_transferred" INTEGER /* Total bytes transferred during the time period */,
  "network_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network analyzed */,
  "user_count" INTEGER /* Number of users during the analysis period */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_analysis_network_id FOREIGN KEY ("network_id") REFERENCES networks ("network_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_selection" (
  "selection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each selection action */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the selection */,
  "network_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network selected */,
  "time_slot" INTEGER /* Time slot of the selection */,
  "selection_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the selection occurred */,
  "selection_reason" TEXT /* Reason for network selection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("selection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_selection_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_selection_network_id FOREIGN KEY ("network_id") REFERENCES networks ("network_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high delay, low bit rate) */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "alert_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alert has been resolved */,
  "threshold_value" TEXT /* Threshold value that triggered the alert */,
  "affected_users" INTEGER /* Number of users affected by the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)
